tasks_completed.changes: Removed `from lib.subagent import is_subagent_context  # noqa: E402` at line 32 (now line 31 is the sys.path.insert); import becomes dead after Edit 3 removes the only call site
tasks_completed.rationale: Dead import causes lint warnings and is misleading after guard removal

tasks_completed.description: Updated module docstring to reflect unconditional legacy cleanup
tasks_completed.changes: Changed 'Main-agent only (subagents are exempt).' to 'Applies to any matching PostToolUse event (subagent or main-agent); legacy grant cleanup is unconditional. Subagent write-grant firewall remains in `hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh` Step 0.'
tasks_completed.rationale: Docstring was stale after M2 extended sentinel grants to subagents

tasks_completed.changes: Replaced `if not is_subagent_context(data): consume_grant_for_posttool(...)` with unconditional `consume_grant_for_posttool(...)` plus updated comment
tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause fix: after M2 extended sentinel grants to subagents, the guard caused asymmetric cleanup — sentinel consumed but legacy grant left on disk for reuse by main agent

tasks_completed.description: Added unconditional legacy grant unlink after sentinel consumption in Bash terminal block
tasks_completed.changes: Added second `if should_consume:` block after sentinel-consume call that unlinks `/tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-{session_id}.json` with FileNotFoundError silently caught
tasks_completed.rationale: Closes whitespace-normalization divergence: sentinel uses tokenized matching (handles 'git   push') but legacy uses literal substring match; when sentinel is consumed, legacy must also be unlinked regardless of literal pattern match

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A — design gap introduced when task 20260521-090200 (M2) extended sentinel grants to subagents without updating posttool cleanup symmetry
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: Before M2, subagents could not use legacy grants, so skipping consume_grant_for_posttool() for subagent context was correct. After M2, sentinel grants authorized subagent Bash commands but the posttool hook cleanup was not updated to match, producing asymmetric cleanup.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: Removing the is_subagent_context guard restores symmetric cleanup: both legacy and sentinel grant files are unlinked on any terminal result, regardless of execution context. The write firewall in userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh Step 0 remains unchanged — subagents still cannot write new grants.

dev.qa_notes: Test with subprocess invocations of posttool-allowlist-consume.py with crafted stdin payloads. AC1: subagent payload (agent_id present, exit_code=0) should unlink both legacy and sentinel. AC2: main-agent payload (no agent_id, exit_code=0) should still unlink legacy (regression). AC5: no legacy grant present should exit 0 with no traceback. AC6: pretool-bash-safety.sh IS_SUBAGENT=1 still blocks legacy-only grant reads (file not modified). See acceptance-criteria-dev-20260524-205811.json for full AC suite.

self_verification.smoke_check: skipped (no executable criteria in context JSON; AC validation is subprocess-based and delegated to QA per BA spec)
self_verification.notes: python3 -m py_compile returned exit 0. Diff verified via git diff --stat (1 file, 16 ins, 5 del). No other files modified.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Finding 1 (BLOCKER): Legacy unlink in Edit 4 uses payload session_id; if subagent session_id differs from orchestrator session_id (grant writer), the legacy grant at /tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-{orchestrator_sid}.json remains. Proposed fix: load sentinel grant, read its session_id, unlink both SIDs. Finding 2 (OBSERVATION_ONLY): Write sentinel branch does not do the new legacy unlink; Write /allow grants could leave legacy file behind.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Finding 1 classified as out_of_scope: BA dispatch explicitly states 'Apply exactly 4 edits to hooks/posttool-allowlist-consume.py'. Implementing codex's proposed fix requires a 5th operation (load sentinel before consume, read session_id, dual-unlink) not authorized by the BA spec. Additionally, Edit 3 (the unconditional consume_grant_for_posttool call) would have the same cross-session-id limitation — if the BA accepted this limitation for Edit 3, it is consistent to accept it for Edit 4. The BA's primary concern (R1-R3) is that consume_grant_for_posttool() is called unconditionally; the cross-SID edge case is a separate future concern. Logged in implementation_notes. Finding 2 classified as out_of_scope: BA scope.excluded explicitly lists sentinel grant consumption logic as unchanged; Write branch is outside the 4 authorized edits.

implementation_notes: Codex Finding 1 (out_of_scope, logged): if a subagent executes with a different session_id than the orchestrator session that wrote the /allow grant, both Edit 3 (consume_grant_for_posttool) and Edit 4 (direct legacy unlink) will silently fail to unlink the correct legacy grant file. This cross-session-id scenario is not addressed by this cycle. The BA's 4-edit scope explicitly limits the fix to what is implemented. A follow-on cycle should consider loading the sentinel grant's session_id before unlinking to cover this case.
Codex Finding 2 (out_of_scope, logged): Write sentinel branch does not have the Edit 4 unconditional legacy unlink. Out of scope per BA spec exclusion of sentinel grant consumption logic.
recommendations: Follow-on cycle: address the cross-SID legacy grant scenario (codex Finding 1) by loading the sentinel grant's session_id before unlinking both /tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-{payload_sid}.json and /tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-{sentinel_sid}.json
Follow-on cycle: evaluate whether the Write sentinel branch also needs a legacy unlink for Write /allow grants (codex Finding 2)
